document_title: Alaska data centers and your power bill, on the grid or off

S1.kicker: ALASKA.AI / No. 10 / THE POWER BILL QUESTION
S1.headline: Will a data center raise your power bill?
S1.body: In Alaska the honest answer splits on one line.

S2.headline: On the grid, or off it.
S2.body: OFF THE GRID. Runs on its own gas. Never touches your wires. ON THE GRID. Shares the wires, and the bill, with you.

S3.headline: The one everyone feared is off the line.
S3.body: STAK's proposed campus sits about 25 miles south of Deadhorse and self-generates a gigawatt or more, roughly 30 percent above urban Alaska's peak. It burns gas behind the meter, with no wire to the Railbelt.

S4.headline: This one plugs into your grid.
S4.body: The state just moved to hand AIDEA about 30 square miles of land near Houston for a data-center district, on the Railbelt. Public comment is open through August 19.
labels: "there are no pre-determined and set land use decisions"

S5.kicker: ON THE GRID, IT CUTS BOTH WAYS
S5.headline: It all turns on the rate deal.
S5.body: RATES COULD FALL. A big anchor load can spread fixed costs, Launch Alaska argues. RATES COULD RISE. Or compete for scarce Railbelt gas.

S6.headline: The case for lower, in one chart.
S6.body: And every 1% drop leaves about $2.5M in fixed costs with nowhere to go. A big anchor load could spread those costs, the argument goes. Could.
labels: PER A LAUNCH ALASKA OP-ED, ADN, APRIL 2026
Chugach-area electric sales, the last 15 years

S7.headline: Alaska is at the very beginning.
S7.body: Eight data centers here, against the nation's leader at 639, as reported by Alaska's News Source. The rules get written while the count is still small.

S8.headline: Build vs ban is the wrong fight.
S8.body: Houston is weighing a ban, postponed to August 13. But the lever is the rate deal. Anchorage adopted guardrails 10 to 2. The statewide bill that would set rate rules, HB 259, still sits in the House Energy Committee.

S9.headline: Before the next data center plugs in, ask one thing. Who writes the rate deal, and does it lower your bill or raise it?
S9.body: On the grid or off it changes everything. What should Alaska require first?

caption:
A data center on the North Slope cannot touch your Anchorage power bill. One in the Mat-Su can.

The difference is not the size of the load, it is one wire. Off-grid projects self-generate and never reach the Railbelt. On-grid ones plug into the same network you pay into. There the answer still splits two ways, and the rate deal decides which.

This deck walks through both Alaska projects in the news, the on-grid versus off-grid split, and why the build-versus-ban fight skips the lever that actually sets your rate.

So what should Alaska require first, a rate deal on every big new load, or nothing until the count grows?

#AlaskaEnergy #DataCenters #Railbelt
